I mean....free money is free money. Why do directorate care? Their job is to secure financing. If the games don't sell...that is because gamers don't know what to like....but shutter the studio anyway because they didn't like that.

Nintendo already was at this point back in the 2000's with one of their biggest IPs. Legend of Zelda. Windwaker "bombed" (didn't match OoT in Sales on their new console), and Nintendo was seriously considering shuttering it for a while because "gamers just didn't like that story anymore"...until Eiji Aonuma basically said....give me one more shot, if it fails I will retire...and Miyamoto said ok. Twilight Princess released a "realistic" LoZ, set in a Dark and Gritty timeline (akin to Majora's Mask)....and it became the best selling LoZ Game until BoTW and later ToTK. The Aonuma gamble saved the franchise, and the LoZ studio from being axed. 

Nintendo was literally ready to walk away from their 3rd largest IP the time....because Cartoon Link didn't catch on with Gamers...and it was clear Gamers didn't like LoZ anymore. (Yes...this is how the suits think).
